fitness-sparse.content:
# THE EXECUTIVE ATHLETE PROGRAM SALES CALL SCRIPT

**Duration:** 45-60 minutes  
**Format:** Phone or Zoom  
**Audience:** Qualified prospects who've consumed lead magnet content

---

## SECTION 1: INTRODUCTION & RAPPORT (0:00-0:01)

"Hey [Name], Marcus here. Good to finally connect with you. How's your day treating you so far?"

[DELIVERY NOTE: Keep energy up but not fake. Sound like you're genuinely interested in them as a person.]

"Before we dive in, I saw on your application that you're running [their company type] — how's business been lately?"

[NOTE: Reference something specific from their application if possible. Keep this under 60 seconds total.]

---

## SECTION 2: FRAME SETTING (0:01-0:02)

"Cool. So here's how I like to run these calls. I'm going to ask you some questions to really understand where you are right now and what you're dealing with. Then if I think I can genuinely help, I'll share what that looks like. If not, I'll point you in the right direction. Either way, you'll get clarity on your next steps. Sound fair?"

[PAUSE for agreement]

"Perfect. And just so you know — this isn't some high-pressure pitch. I work with 12 clients max at any given time, so I'm pretty selective about who I take on. I genuinely want to understand your situation first."

---

## SECTION 3: PROBLEM ISOLATION (0:02-0:03)

"So tell me — what's the biggest challenge you're facing with your health and fitness right now?"

[DELIVERY NOTE: SHUT UP and listen. Do not interrupt. Do not solve yet. Let them talk for 30-60 seconds.]

"And how long has this been going on?"

[NOTE: They'll usually give you a surface answer first. Your job is to go 10x deeper in the next section.]

---

## SECTION 4: PROBLEM UNDERSTANDING — DEEP PROBING (0:03-0:11)

"When you say [repeat their exact words], what does that actually look like day-to-day? Give me a specific example from this past week."

[LISTEN, then follow up with chunking questions:]

"So you're working [X hours] and traveling [X frequency] — walk me through what happens when you try to work out. What's the actual sequence of events?"

"What's that costing you? Not just in terms of your health, but in energy, confidence, how you show up for your family?"

[CONSEQUENCE QUESTIONS:]

"How is this affecting your business performance? Your energy during long days?"

"What about your family? How does this impact your time with your kids, your relationship with your wife?"

"If nothing changes in the next 12 months — if you're in this exact same spot a year from now — what does that look like for you?"

[FEELING QUESTIONS:]

"How does it feel when you catch your reflection in the elevator mirror each morning?"

"What goes through your head when you see other executives at company events who are clearly in better shape?"

"What's the worst part about this whole situation for you personally?"

[DELIVERY NOTE: This is the most critical section. Spend 8-10 minutes here. The more pain you uncover, the easier everything else becomes. Use their exact words back to them.]

---

## SECTION 5: DOUBT CREATION (0:11-0:12)

"So you've been trying to [repeat their current approach] — and how's that been working for you?"

[NOTE: They'll usually laugh or say "obviously not great."]

"Right. Why do you think [their approach] hasn't worked so far? What's been missing?"

[LISTEN for their answer, then:]

"That makes sense. Most executives try to force generic fitness programs into their schedule instead of building something around their actual life."

---

## SECTION 6: PAST SOLUTION ANALYSIS (0:12-0:15)

"What else have you tried to solve this? What trainers, programs, apps, whatever — walk me through your fitness graveyard."

[LISTEN, then probe each one:]

"What worked about [specific program]? What didn't? Why did you stop?"

"Looking back, what was missing from those approaches?"

"What's different about your situation now compared to when you tried those things?"

[NOTE: Listen for patterns. Usually they've tried generic solutions that didn't account for travel, schedule changes, or business demands.]

---

## SECTION 7: SUPPORT ASSESSMENT (0:15-0:17)

"Who's helping you with this right now? Do you have a trainer, coach, nutritionist — anyone?"

"How are you making decisions about your health and fitness — just figuring it out on your own?"

"When you hit obstacles or your schedule explodes, who do you turn to for guidance?"

[NOTE: Most will say they're going it alone. This sets up the need for expert guidance.]

---

## SECTION 8: DESIRE EXPLORATION (0:17-0:17)

"Alright, let's flip this. If we could wave a magic wand and solve this completely — what would your health and fitness look like 6 months from now?"

"Walk me through your ideal day. You wake up, and what's different?"

"How would that feel compared to where you are now?"

"What kind of example would you be setting for your kids?"

"Who would you become if this was handled?"

[NOTE: Let them paint their own Heaven Island. Reference "executive athlete" language if they don't use it naturally.]

---

## SECTION 9: WHY NOW / COST OF INACTION (0:17-0:21)

"Let me ask you something. You said this has been going on for [timeframe they mentioned]. What has that already cost you?"

"I mean in terms of energy, confidence, time with your family, even business performance?"

"And if nothing changes — if you're sitting here having this exact same conversation 12 months from now — what does that look like?"

"Is that acceptable to you?"

[PAUSE. Let them sit with that.]

"What are you most afraid will happen if you don't solve this?"

[NOTE: This should feel like holding up a mirror, not manipulation. You're reflecting back what they already know.]

---

## SECTION 10: CLARITY TIE-DOWN (0:21-0:23)

"Let me make sure I'm understanding you correctly. You're dealing with [summarize their problem], you've tried [their past attempts], but they haven't worked because [their stated reasons]. What you really want is [their desired outcome], and if you don't solve this, you're looking at [consequences they mentioned]. Does that sound right? Did I miss anything?"

[WAIT for confirmation]

"Got it. And you're handling this on your own right now with no real system or support."

---

## SECTION 11: DISCOVERY COMMITMENT (0:23-0:24)

"Based on everything you've shared, I actually think I can help. Would you like to hear how?"

[NOTE: They'll almost always say yes. If they hesitate, that's fine — enthusiasm comes during the pitch.]

---

## SECTION 12: TRANSITION TO PITCH (0:24-0:26)

"Here's what I've noticed from everything you've shared. The reason the trainers, apps, and programs haven't worked isn't because you lack discipline or willpower. It's because they were all designed for people with predictable schedules and unlimited time."

"You're trying to use a corner store system to run a Fortune 500 operation. Of course it doesn't work."

"What I've built — The Performance Stack — addresses the root cause. It's specifically designed for executives who travel constantly, work 60-hour weeks, and have unpredictable schedules. Here's how it works."

---

## SECTION 13: THE PITCH — 3-PHASE SOLUTION (0:26-0:35)

**"Phase 1: Assessment"**

"First thing we do is create your custom baseline through 3 video calls plus bloodwork review. We map out your actual schedule, your travel patterns, your stress points, your home setup — everything. This isn't some generic assessment. We're building around your real life."

"Remember when you said [reference their specific scheduling challenge]? This is where we solve that. By the end of Phase 1, you have a foundation that actually fits your reality."

**"Phase 2: Rebuild"**

"Then we get your first 15-20 pound loss while building your strength baseline. This is 8 weeks of in-home strength training plus a nutrition protocol that works with business dinners and travel."

"You mentioned [reference their energy/confidence issue] — this is where that changes. You're not just losing weight, you're building the physical foundation that gives you energy all day."

**"Phase 3: Scale"**

"Finally, we create your complete transformation plus maintenance system. 12 weeks of progressive loading plus travel protocols. This is where you go from The Treadmill Desk CEO to The Executive Athlete."

"You know how you said you want to [reference their desired outcome]? This is where that becomes your reality. You have a system that travels with you, adapts to your schedule, and maintains your results no matter how crazy business gets."

[NOTE: Throughout the pitch, reference THEIR specific words from discovery. Make it feel custom, not scripted.]

---

## SECTION 14: TEMPERATURE CHECK (0:36-0:39)

"So on a scale of 1 to 10, how does this feel so far?"

**If 7+:** "What would make it a 10?"
**If 5-6:** "What's holding you back?"
**If below 5:** "Sounds like this might not be the right fit, and that's totally fine. What specifically doesn't resonate?"

[NOTE: The temperature check reveals hidden objections. Handle whatever comes up before moving forward.]

---

## SECTION 15: TRANSITION TO CLOSE (0:39-0:40)

"Great. So would you like to hear what it looks like to get started?"

[NOTE: This is asking permission to present the offer details — NOT the close itself.]

---

## SECTION 16: DELIVERABLES & SUPPORT (0:40-0:41)

"Here's exactly what you get: Custom training program designed around your home office setup, nutrition protocol that works with business dinners and travel, weekly video reviews where I personally analyze your form and progress, biweekly 1-on-1 strategy calls, and private Slack access for immediate support."

"Everything's delivered through a private client portal. The weekly video reviews happen on your schedule — you film key exercises, I give detailed feedback within 24 hours. The calls are scheduled around your availability."

"You'll never be stuck wondering what to do next. Any questions about how it works?"

---

## SECTION 17: INVESTMENT REVEAL (0:41-0:42)

"The investment for The Executive Athlete Program is $12,000 for 6 months."

[DELIVERY NOTE: State the price and STOP TALKING. Let them process. Do not fill the silence.]

"We also offer a payment plan if that works better for your cash flow."

"To put that in perspective — you mentioned [their specific cost of staying stuck]. This pays for itself when you consider the energy increase, the confidence boost, and avoiding the health problems that cost six figures down the road."

---

## SECTION 18: OBJECTION HANDLING

### "I need to think about it"
"I totally understand. Can I ask — what specifically do you need to think about?"

[LISTEN, then:]

"In my experience, 'I need to think about it' usually means there's a specific concern I haven't addressed. What is it?"

### "I can't afford it"
"I hear you. Let me ask — is it that the money literally doesn't exist, or that you're not sure if it's worth the investment?"

**If literal:** "Would the payment plan make this possible?"

**If value:** "What would it be worth to you to go from [their current state] to [their desired state] in 6 months? You mentioned [their cost of staying stuck] — this solves that."

### "I've tried coaching before"
"What specifically didn't work about it?"

[LISTEN, then:]

"That makes sense. The difference with The Performance Stack is [address their specific gap]. What was missing before was a system designed for your exact constraints."

### "I need to talk to my spouse"
"Absolutely — it's important to be aligned. What specifically do you think they'd want to know?"

"Would it help if I sent them a summary of what we discussed?"

### "I don't have time to train"
"Right, that's exactly what Dan thought. He was traveling 2 weeks per month. But here's what he discovered: The Performance Stack isn't about finding more time — it's about syncing training to the time you actually have. Twenty minutes between meetings is enough when you have the right system."

### "Online coaching can't replace in-person"
"I get it. But here's the difference: most online coaching gives you generic programs. The Performance Stack is specifically designed for your constraints. When Rick started, he'd failed with three different in-person trainers because none accounted for his travel schedule. Once he had a system built for his reality, his A1C dropped from 6.2 to 5.4."

### "I've tried this before and it didn't stick"
"Right, because you were using the wrong system. It's like trying to run your company with software designed for a different industry. The reason you haven't succeeded isn't discipline — it's because you've been forcing generic solutions into an executive lifestyle."

---

## SECTION 19: THE CLOSE (0:42+)

**If ready:**
"Awesome. Let me get you set up right now. I'll send you the onboarding link and we'll schedule your first assessment call."

**If needs time:**
"No pressure at all. I'll send you a summary of everything we discussed. When's a good time to follow up — tomorrow or Thursday?"

**If no:**
"I respect that. If anything changes, you know where to find me. In the meantime, keep doing what you're doing and I hope you find the right solution."

[NOTE: Never beg, negotiate on price, or create fake urgency. "I don't chase. You jump, I jump."]

---

## SECTION 20: POST-CALL SUMMARY

**Template to fill out after each call:**

- **Prospect name:**
- **Key problem identified:**
- **What resonated most:**
- **Objections raised:**
- **Outcome (closed / follow-up / not a fit):**
- **Follow-up date:**
- **Notes:**

---

### DELIVERY PRINCIPLES

1. **Discovery is everything** — Spend 50% of the call understanding their problem
2. **Use their words** — Reference their exact phrases throughout the pitch
3. **Don't chase** — If they're not ready, that's fine. Desperation kills deals
4. **Handle objections with questions** — "What specifically..." reveals the real concern
5. **Silence is powerful** — After stating the price, shut up and let them process
6. **Be human** — This is a conversation between two professionals, not a script reading

### VOICE REMINDERS

- **Direct, no-BS, tactical**
- **Never use hype words** like "incredible" or "amazing"
- **Sound like a smart professional** having a real conversation
- **Be empathetic without being soft**
- **Confidence without arrogance**

fitness-sparse.content:
# HOMEPAGE COPY FOR REED PERFORMANCE

---

## HERO SECTION

**Headline:** How Male Executives Are Getting in the Best Shape of Their Lives Using a 3-Layer System That Syncs to Their 60-Hour Work Week

**Subheadline:** Stop buying Peloton bikes that collect dust and hiring trainers you cancel on. The Performance Stack is designed for executives who travel constantly, work unpredictable hours, and need results that fit their reality — not someone else's schedule.

**Primary CTA:** Apply for The Executive Athlete Program

**Hero Image Suggestion:** Professional executive in a home office setting, mid-workout with minimal equipment, looking confident and energized — not a generic gym photo.

---

## PROBLEM SECTION

### The Problem

You catch your reflection in the elevator mirror every morning and wince. By 3pm, you're running on fumes while your younger VP still has energy to spare. You've bought the Peloton, hired three different trainers, and downloaded every fitness app — but your 60-hour weeks and constant travel make every program impossible to follow.

**Sound familiar?**

- You work 60+ hours a week but can't find 30 minutes for consistent training
- Every trainer gives you the same cookie-cutter program that ignores your travel schedule
- You're exhausted by 3pm and dread your annual physical
- You've tried everything but nothing sticks because nothing fits your actual life
- You're skipping your son's baseball games because you're too tired to be present

Here's what nobody tells you: every fitness program fails busy executives because they're built for people with predictable schedules, not leaders running companies. You don't need more willpower — you need a system designed for your reality.

---

## SOLUTION SECTION

### The Performance Stack

The Performance Stack is a 3-layer system that syncs training, nutrition, and recovery to the rhythm of your 60-hour work week. Instead of forcing your schedule around a generic program, we build the program around your actual constraints.

**Phase 1: Assessment (3 video calls + bloodwork review)**
We create your custom baseline through comprehensive health analysis, schedule mapping, and travel pattern assessment. No cookie-cutter approaches — everything is built around your real life.

**Phase 2: Rebuild (8 weeks of in-home strength + nutrition protocol)**
You get your first 15-20 pound loss while building strength using custom home workout programs and travel-friendly nutrition guidelines. Weekly video reviews keep you accountable without wasted time.

**Phase 3: Scale (12 weeks of progressive loading + travel protocols)**
Complete transformation with a maintenance system that travels with you. Advanced training progressions, hotel gym mastery, and business dinner navigation strategies ensure you never lose progress.

**Why this works when everything else fails:** Generic programs assume you have 2 hours daily and never leave town. The Performance Stack accounts for client dinners, business travel, and unpredictable schedules. When your flight gets delayed, you have protocols. When deals heat up, you have contingencies.

---

## RESULTS SECTION

### The Transformation

**From The Treadmill Desk CEO to The Executive Athlete**

Stop avoiding photos at company events. Stop making excuses about "starting Monday." Stop feeling like a fraud who can optimize businesses but not his own body.

Become the executive who takes his body as seriously as his P&L. Walk into any room confident and strong. Have energy that lasts from 5 AM to 9 PM. Take your shirt off at the beach without sucking in.

**Real Results:**

Dan M., SaaS CEO, was burned out and 42 pounds over target. He hadn't trained consistently in 3 years and kept canceling on trainers due to travel. Using The Performance Stack, he lost 42 pounds in 6 months while traveling 2 weeks per month and now squats 2x bodyweight. "Marcus is the only trainer who actually gets what my life is like."

Rick P., private equity partner, had a prediabetic A1C of 6.2 and was facing medication. After 6 months with our system, his A1C dropped to 5.4 and he lost 28 pounds. "My doctor couldn't believe the numbers."

**Your guarantee:** Lose 20 pounds in the first 90 days while following the protocol, or I work with you free until you do.

---

## ABOUT SECTION

### About Marcus Reed

I help busy executives get in the best shape of their lives without leaving their home office.

In 2021, I was training 12 hours a day at my gym and still barely breaking even. I was burnt out, gaining weight, and resenting clients. During COVID, a client asked if I could train him over Zoom. I said yes, and realized I could help just as many people without a physical location.

I closed the gym in 2022 and went fully online. Now I coach executives remotely with customized home programs using The Performance Stack — the system I developed after 11 years of training 340 clients and discovering what actually works for busy leaders.

I'm NASM CPT and Precision Nutrition L1 certified, but more importantly, I understand what it's like to sacrifice your own health while building a business. That's why I created a system that enhances your performance instead of competing with it.

---

## NOT FOR YOU IF... SECTION

### This is NOT for you if:

- You want a quick fix or magic pill solution
- You're not willing to follow a structured system for 6 months
- You make excuses instead of taking responsibility for your health
- You're looking for the cheapest option available
- You expect results without putting in consistent effort
- You're not ready to invest $12,000 to solve this problem once and for all

If that's you, please don't apply. This program is for executives who are serious about transformation, not those looking for shortcuts.

---

## THIS IS FOR YOU IF... SECTION

### This IS for you if:

- You run a company making $500K+ and your health has taken a backseat
- You travel frequently for business and need a system that adapts
- You've tried 3+ fitness approaches that failed because of your schedule
- You're ready to treat your body as seriously as you treat your business
- You want to set an example for your kids about what's possible
- You understand that expert guidance accelerates results and prevents costly mistakes

You're not looking for more information — you're looking for the right system and the accountability to implement it.

---

## FINAL CTA SECTION

### Ready to Become The Executive Athlete?

You have two choices:

Take what you've learned and try to figure it out yourself — spending years on trial and error while your health continues to decline.

Or apply for The Executive Athlete Program and get the proven system that transforms executives in 6 months.

I only work with 12 clients at a time because each person gets custom protocols and direct access to me. When those spots are filled, they're filled.

The investment is $12,000 for 6 months — less than what most executives spend on a single business trip, but the investment that transforms everything else.

**Your transformation starts with an application.**

Click below to apply. I'll personally review your application and if you're a good fit, we'll schedule a call to discuss your specific situation and goals.

Stop waiting for the perfect time. Stop making excuses about your schedule. Stop settling for a body that doesn't match your business success.

**[APPLY FOR THE EXECUTIVE ATHLETE PROGRAM]**

---

*Marcus Reed*  
*Founder, Reed Performance*  
*11 years experience, 340 clients coached*  
*NASM CPT, Precision Nutrition L1*

fitness-sparse.content:
# About Marcus Reed

## The Mirror Moment That Changes Everything

You know that split second when you catch your reflection in the elevator mirror each morning? That moment when you realize the successful executive staring back at you doesn't match the body you're carrying around? That was me in 2021 — running a gym, training clients 12 hours a day, and somehow gaining weight while preaching fitness to others.

## From Burnt-Out Trainer to Executive Athlete

Here's what nobody tells you about the fitness industry: most trainers have never run a real business. They don't understand what it's like to work 60-hour weeks, travel constantly, or navigate client dinners while trying to stay in shape. I learned this the hard way.

In 2021, I was training 12 hours a day at my gym and still barely breaking even after lease and payroll. I was burnt out, gaining weight, and resenting clients. The irony wasn't lost on me — I was helping others get in shape while my own health was falling apart. I was The Treadmill Desk CEO, just like the executives I'd later help transform.

Then COVID hit, and a client asked if I could train him over Zoom. I said yes, expecting it to be a temporary solution. Instead, I realized I could help just as many people without a physical location — and more importantly, I could design programs that actually fit their real lives instead of forcing them into a gym schedule that didn't work.

That moment changed everything. I closed the gym in 2022 and went fully online. I developed The Performance Stack — a 3-layer system that syncs training, nutrition, and recovery to the rhythm of a 60-hour work week. Now I coach executives remotely with customized home programs, and they get better results than they ever did with traditional approaches.

I went from being burnt out and overweight to becoming The Executive Athlete — someone who takes his body as seriously as his P&L. More importantly, I discovered how to help other executives make the same transformation without sacrificing their business performance.

## What I Believe

**Generic fitness programs are designed for people who don't exist.** Most programs assume you have 90 minutes a day, a predictable schedule, and no business travel. That's not reality for executives running companies. You need a system built around your constraints, not despite them.

**Your real problem isn't willpower — it's that you don't have a system designed for executive life.** You've been trying to force square pegs into round holes. The moment you stop blaming yourself for "lack of discipline" and start addressing the systemic issues, everything changes.

**You can't afford NOT to invest in your health right now.** Every quarter you delay is another quarter of declining energy, focus, and performance. Your body is your most important business asset — if you won't invest in maintaining it, you're being fiscally irresponsible.

**Online coaching with the right system beats generic in-person training every time.** Location doesn't determine effectiveness — customization does. I'd rather coach someone remotely with protocols designed for their specific challenges than train them in person with a cookie-cutter program.

**The best time to start was three years ago — the second best time is today.** "After this quarter" never comes. There will always be another deal, another crisis, another excuse. The executives who transform are the ones who make their health non-negotiable right now.

**Taking care of yourself isn't selfish — it's the most important thing you can do for everyone who depends on you.** Your family, your employees, your clients — they all need you operating at peak performance. You can't pour from an empty cup.

## The Numbers

In 11 years of training, I've coached 340 clients and earned my NASM CPT and Precision Nutrition L1 certifications. I've seen what works and what doesn't when it comes to executive fitness. The executives who succeed are the ones who stop trying to fit their lives into generic programs and start using systems designed for their reality.

My clients lose an average of 30 pounds in 6 months while maintaining their business performance. They travel 2 weeks per month and stay consistent. They navigate client dinners without derailing their progress. They have more energy at 5 PM than most people have at 9 AM.

## Beyond the Business

When I'm not coaching executives, you'll find me running the trails around Austin, reading everything from business strategy to nutrition research, or perfecting my barbecue technique. I'm married with two kids, and they keep me grounded in what really matters.

I was raised in Ohio by a single mom who worked two jobs and modeled relentless work ethic. That foundation taught me that success isn't about shortcuts — it's about building systems that work and executing them consistently. The same principles that build successful businesses build successful transformations.

I do this work because I was the fat kid nobody believed in, and now I help people who've given up on themselves prove their own critics wrong. There's nothing more satisfying than watching a successful executive realize they can have the body that matches their business achievements.

## Ready to Become The Executive Athlete?

If you're tired of catching your reflection and feeling disappointed, if you're done with programs that fall apart the moment you travel, if you want a system that actually fits your life instead of fighting against it — let's talk.

The Executive Athlete Program isn't for everyone. It's for executives who are ready to invest $12,000 in solving this problem once and for all. It's for leaders who understand that their health is the foundation of everything else they do.

**[Apply for The Executive Athlete Program →]**

Your business success proves you know how to build systems that work. Now let's build one for your body.

fitness-sparse.content:
# WELCOME EMAIL SEQUENCE FOR REED PERFORMANCE

---

## EMAIL 1: WELCOME + DELIVERY
**Send:** Immediately after opt-in  
**Purpose:** Deliver lead magnet, set expectations, build immediate trust

**Subject Line:** Your 15-minute workout is here (plus what's coming next)  
**Alt Subject:** The Executive's 15-Minute Workout + 6 more emails that matter

**Preview Text:** Download your workout + see what's coming this week

**Body Copy:**

You just downloaded The Executive's 15-Minute Workout.

Here's the link: [DOWNLOAD LINK]

This isn't another generic HIIT routine. It's designed specifically for executives who need maximum impact in minimum time. You can do it in your home office between meetings.

Over the next week, I'm sending you 6 more emails that cut through the fitness industry bullshit and show you what actually works for busy executives.

Here's what's coming:

• Tomorrow: Why I closed my gym and went fully online (and why it was the best business decision I ever made)
• Day 3: The real reason every fitness program has failed you
• Day 5: Why "I don't have time" is actually a systems problem, not a time problem
• Day 7: Two executives, same schedule, completely different results
• Day 9: What happens if you do nothing for the next 6 months
• Day 11: How to become The Executive Athlete (if you're ready)

Each email takes 2 minutes to read and gives you one tactical insight you can use immediately.

No fluff. No hype. Just what works.

Talk soon,

Marcus

P.S. Try the 15-minute workout today. Time it. You'll be surprised how much you can accomplish when the system is designed for your reality.

**CTA:** Download and try the workout

---

## EMAIL 2: ORIGIN STORY → HELL ISLAND TO HEAVEN ISLAND
**Send:** Day 1-2  
**Purpose:** Share Marcus's transformation story, build trust through vulnerability

**Subject Line:** Why I closed my profitable gym  
**Alt Subject:** From 12-hour days to 15-minute workouts

**Preview Text:** The day I realized location wasn't the problem...

**Body Copy:**

In 2021, I was training 12 hours a day at my gym.

Barely breaking even after lease and payroll. Gaining weight. Resenting clients.

Sound familiar?

I was caught in the same trap most executives face: working harder but getting worse results. My own health was falling apart while I preached fitness to others.

Every morning, I'd catch my reflection in the gym mirrors and think, "You're a fraud."

By 3pm, I was running on fumes. Missing family dinners because I was too exhausted to be present. Telling myself I'd focus on my health "after the business was stable."

But the business was never stable enough. There was always another crisis, another client emergency, another reason to push my health to next quarter.

Then COVID hit.

A client asked if I could train him over Zoom. I almost said no. "Online coaching isn't real coaching," I thought.

But I said yes.

And something clicked.

He got better results training from his home office than he ever did in my gym. No commute time. No waiting for equipment. No excuses about traffic or parking.

I realized the problem was never location. It was having a system designed for his actual life.

So I closed the gym in 2022. Went fully online.

Now I coach executives remotely with customized home programs. I'm in the best shape of my life. My clients get results they never thought possible.

The difference? I stopped trying to force square pegs into round holes.

I started building systems around reality, not ideals.

Tomorrow, I'll show you why every fitness program you've tried has failed you.

(Hint: It's not your fault.)

Marcus

**CTA:** Hit reply and tell me: What's your biggest struggle with staying consistent?

---

## EMAIL 3: CORE BELIEF #1 — YOUR CURRENT METHOD IS THE PROBLEM
**Send:** Day 3-4  
**Purpose:** Challenge false belief about generic programs, introduce systems thinking

**Subject Line:** You've been using a corner store system for a Fortune 500 problem  
**Alt Subject:** Why willpower is a terrible business strategy

**Preview Text:** The real reason every program fails busy executives...

**Body Copy:**

You wouldn't run a $2 million company using systems designed for a corner store.

Different complexity. Different demands. Different constraints.

Yet most executives try to get in shape using programs designed for people with 2-hour gym windows who never travel for business.

Here's what I see constantly:

Executive downloads MyFitnessPal. Works great for 2 weeks. Then he's in Chicago closing a deal, eating client dinners, and the app becomes useless.

Executive hires a local trainer. Sessions work fine until travel season hits. Cancels 3 sessions in a row. Trainer gets frustrated. Executive feels guilty. Relationship ends.

Executive buys a Peloton. Uses it twice. Now it's an expensive clothes rack because 45-minute classes don't fit between board meetings.

The problem isn't willpower. It's not discipline. It's not motivation.

The problem is using the wrong system for the job.

You already know this in business. You don't use the same strategy for a startup that you'd use for a Fortune 500 company. You customize based on resources, constraints, and goals.

But somehow, when it comes to fitness, you think one-size-fits-all should work.

It doesn't.

You need a system designed for executives who:
• Travel 40% of the time
• Work 60-hour weeks
• Have unpredictable schedules
• Eat business dinners twice a week
• Can't commit to fixed gym times

That's why I developed The Performance Stack.

It's not another fitness program. It's a business system for health that syncs training, nutrition, and recovery to the rhythm of your actual work week.

Tomorrow, I'll show you why "I don't have time" is actually a systems problem, not a time problem.

Marcus

**CTA:** Reply and tell me: What's the biggest constraint in your schedule that kills your consistency?

---

## EMAIL 4: CORE BELIEF #2 — THE REAL PROBLEM IS NOT WHAT YOU THINK
**Send:** Day 5-6  
**Purpose:** Reframe the real problem, introduce The Performance Stack solution

**Subject Line:** "I don't have time" is a systems problem  
**Alt Subject:** Why your schedule isn't the enemy

**Preview Text:** The real problem hiding behind every excuse...

**Body Copy:**

"I don't have time to train."

I hear this constantly from executives making $300K+ who somehow find time for:
• 2-hour client dinners
• Golf with prospects
• Industry conferences
• Networking events

You have time. You just don't have a system that works with your time.

Here's the real problem: you've been trying to fit your life into someone else's fitness program instead of building a fitness program around your life.

Every trainer gives you the same advice: "Just wake up at 5 AM and hit the gym before work."

But what happens when you're in a different time zone? When you had client calls until midnight? When your hotel gym closes at 9 PM?

The system breaks. You feel like a failure. You start over Monday.

The real problem isn't your schedule. It's that no one has built a system that accounts for:

• Business travel 2 weeks per month
• Client dinners that wreck your meal plan
• 12-hour days that leave you exhausted
• Hotel gyms with broken equipment
• Jet lag that destroys your sleep

That's why I created The Performance Stack.

It's a 3-layer system that syncs training, nutrition, and recovery to the rhythm of a 60-hour work week.

Layer 1: Training that adapts to your location and available time
Layer 2: Nutrition strategies for business dinners and travel
Layer 3: Recovery protocols that work with high-stress demands

When your flight gets delayed, you don't skip training. You pull up your hotel room workout.

When you have a client dinner, you don't abandon your nutrition plan. You use the business dinner protocol.

When you're jet-lagged, you don't push through exhaustion. You follow the recovery system.

For the first time, you have a system that works with your reality instead of against it.

Tomorrow, I'll show you proof this actually works.

Marcus

**CTA:** Hit reply: What's the #1 thing that always derails your fitness plans?

---

## EMAIL 5: CORE BELIEF #5 — WHY THIS APPROACH WORKS
**Send:** Day 7-8  
**Purpose:** Provide social proof and case studies, prove the method works

**Subject Line:** How Dan lost 42 pounds while traveling 2 weeks per month  
**Alt Subject:** Two executives, same constraints, different results

**Preview Text:** The SaaS CEO who thought online coaching was impossible...

**Body Copy:**

Dan M. runs a SaaS company. Travels 2 weeks every month. Works 60-hour weeks.

He'd tried everything:
• Local trainers (canceled too often)
• Gym memberships (never used them)
• Fitness apps (abandoned after 2 weeks)

"I need someone in person for accountability," he told me. "Online coaching can't work with my schedule."

He was wrong.

Six months later: 42 pounds down, squats 2x bodyweight, travels more than ever but stays on track.

"Marcus is the only trainer who actually gets what my life is like."

Here's what changed:

Instead of forcing Dan into a generic program, we built The Performance Stack around his reality.

When he's in Austin: 20-minute home office workouts between meetings.

When he's traveling: Hotel room protocols that work with any equipment.

When he has client dinners: Strategic nutrition guidelines that don't require saying no to business opportunities.

The system travels with him. Adapts to his schedule. Makes consistency inevitable instead of heroic.

# CONTENT ANGLE LIBRARY: REED PERFORMANCE

**Coach:** Marcus Reed

**Niche:** In-home fitness coaching for busy executives  
**Target Audience:** Male executives age 40-55 who run their own companies, make $300K+, and haven't prioritized their health in years

## SECTION 1: MIDDLE OF FUNNEL (MOF) — 93+ ANGLES

### A. IDENTITY & MINDSET (13 angles)

| # | Hook (first line) | Target Belief (1-6) | Evidence Type | Format | Brief Outline (2-3 sentences) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Your clients are a mirror of your standards — if they're broke and flaky, look in the mirror. | Belief #6 | Universal Story | Video/Post | Executives who accept mediocrity in their health attract mediocre business relationships. When you become The Executive Athlete, you naturally attract higher-caliber clients and partners who respect excellence. |
| 2 | If you hesitate to invest $12,000 in your health, your clients will hesitate to invest in your services. | Belief #4 | Cross-Domain Reference | Post/Reel | Your investment behavior teaches others how to value your offerings. Executives who invest decisively in themselves create a culture of decisive investment around them. |
| 3 | How you spend money signals who you are — $300 on supplements but won't invest $12,000 in a system that works. | Belief #4 | Universal Analogy | Reel/Story | Small, ineffective purchases feel safe but compound into massive waste. One strategic investment eliminates years of scattered spending on solutions that don't work. |
| 4 | Mediocrity is contagious — surround yourself with executives who prioritize health and you'll naturally follow. | Belief #6 | Authority Reference | Post/Video | Jim Rohn's principle that you become the average of the five people you spend the most time with applies to health habits. Join a community of Executive Athletes instead of accepting the "busy executive" excuse culture. |
| 5 | Confused prospects don't buy — if you can't clearly explain why you're worth $300K salary, you need clarity training. | Belief #1 | Universal Story | Video/Post | Executives who can't articulate their value struggle with confidence in all areas, including health decisions. Clarity about your worth makes investing in yourself obvious, not optional. |
| 6 | Millionaires track their body composition weekly — broke executives track Netflix episodes. | Belief #3 | Internal Case Study | Reel/Post | Successful executives treat their body metrics like business KPIs because they understand that physical performance drives business performance. What you measure consistently improves consistently. |
| 7 | Your relationship with money dictates your business results — cheap health decisions create cheap business thinking. | Belief #4 | Cross-Domain Reference | Video/Post | Executives who make investment decisions from scarcity in one area create scarcity thinking in all areas. Strategic health investment rewires your brain for abundance and long-term thinking. |
| 8 | How you spend your first hour reveals your real priorities — email or exercise? | Belief #3 | Universal Analogy | Reel/Story | Your morning routine is your priority declaration to the world. Executives who start with their health have energy for everything else; those who start with email are drained by 10 AM. |
| 9 | Fast decisions separate top performers — you decided to buy your company in weeks but can't decide on your health? | Belief #6 | Personal Story | Post/Video | Business leaders make million-dollar decisions quickly because they trust their judgment, but second-guess $12,000 health investments. The same decision-making skills that built your business apply to your body. |
| 10 | You can't hit a target you can't see — "get healthier" isn't a goal, it's a wish. | Belief #1 | Universal Analogy | Video/Post | Vague health goals produce vague results just like vague business goals. The Performance Stack gives you specific, measurable targets that your executive brain can actually execute against. |

## SECTION 4: SHORT-FORM HOOKS (20 scroll-stopping hooks)

**CONTENT PRODUCTION NOTES:**

1. **Voice Consistency:** All content should maintain Marcus's tactical, no-BS tone without hype language
2. **Niche Specificity:** Every piece should clearly target male executives, not generic fitness audience
3. **Belief Integration:** Each angle should advance one of the 6 core beliefs systematically
4. **Call-to-Action Variety:** Mix soft CTAs (engagement) with hard CTAs (application/download)
5. **Platform Optimization:** Adjust format and length for each platform's best practices
6. **Measurement:** Track which angles generate highest engagement and conversion rates
7. **Iteration:** Test different hooks for the same belief to optimize performance

This content library provides 118+ unique angles that systematically move prospects from awareness to conversion while maintaining Marcus Reed's authentic voice and addressing the specific challenges of busy executives.

fitness-sparse.content:
# GHL CHAT/SMS AUTOMATION SEQUENCE
**Coach:** Marcus Reed | **Business:** Reed Performance | **Platform:** Instagram DM

---

#### STEP 1: Initial Response to DM Keyword

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact sends DM with keyword "READY" or "EXECUTIVE" or "ATHLETE"

**Time Delay:** None (immediate response)

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact responds: → Go to Step 2
> - IF no response after 24 hours: → Go to Step 8 (Follow-up)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: lead-source-instagram] [+Add: executive-fitness-interest]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: new-lead]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Hey there! I saw you're interested in executive fitness. Quick question - are you currently running your own company and making $300K+ annually? Just want to make sure I can actually help before we dive in.

---

#### STEP 2: Revenue Qualification

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact replies YES to previous message

**Time Delay:** None (immediate response)

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact confirms revenue qualification: → Go to Step 3
> - IF contact doesn't meet revenue requirement: → Go to Step 9 (Disqualify)
> - IF no response after 12 hours: → Go to Step 8 (Follow-up)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: revenue-qualified]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: qualified-lead]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Perfect. Here's the thing - most executives I work with have tried 3+ different approaches to get in shape and failed. They've bought Pelotons that collect dust, hired trainers they cancel on, downloaded apps they abandon after 2 weeks.

Sound familiar? And if so, what's been your biggest frustration with previous attempts?

---

#### STEP 3: Problem Identification

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact shares their fitness frustrations/failures

**Time Delay:** None (immediate response)

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact shows engagement: → Go to Step 4
> - IF contact gives short/unengaged response: → Go to Step 10 (Re-engage)
> - IF no response after 12 hours: → Go to Step 8 (Follow-up)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: problem-identified]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: engaged-lead]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

I get it. The problem isn't your willpower - it's that every program is designed for people with 2-hour gym windows who never travel for business.

Quick question: How many weeks per month are you traveling for work? And when you're home, how many hours are you typically working per day?

I need to understand your real constraints before I can tell you if I can help.

---

#### STEP 4: Schedule Assessment

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact provides travel/work schedule details

**Time Delay:** None (immediate response)

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF schedule fits program parameters: → Go to Step 5
> - IF schedule too demanding for program: → Go to Step 9 (Disqualify)
> - IF no response after 12 hours: → Go to Step 8 (Follow-up)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: schedule-assessed]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: qualified-prospect]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Got it. Here's what I've learned working with 340 clients over 11 years - executives need a system that syncs to their reality, not against it.

That's why I created The Performance Stack. It's specifically designed for executives who work 60-hour weeks and travel constantly.

One more question: If you could lose 30 pounds in the next 6 months without missing a single business meeting, what would that be worth to you?

---

#### STEP 5: Value Assessment & Investment Readiness

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact expresses value/desire for transformation

**Time Delay:** None (immediate response)

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact shows investment readiness: → Go to Step 6
> - IF contact hesitant about investment: → Go to Step 11 (Value Building)
> - IF no response after 12 hours: → Go to Step 8 (Follow-up)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: value-identified]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: hot-prospect]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Here's the reality: The Executive Athlete Program is $12,000 for 6 months. That's less than what most executives spend on a single business trip.

But before we talk details, I need to know you're serious. Are you ready to invest that level to solve this problem once and for all? Or are you still shopping around for the cheapest option?

Because if you're looking for cheap, I'm not your guy. But if you want results that last, keep reading.

---

#### STEP 6: Program Overview & Case Study

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact confirms investment readiness

**Time Delay:** None (immediate response)

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact shows strong interest: → Go to Step 7
> - IF contact has objections: → Go to Step 12 (Objection Handling)
> - IF no response after 12 hours: → Go to Step 8 (Follow-up)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: investment-ready] [+Add: program-interest]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: ready-to-close]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Perfect. Let me tell you about Dan M. - SaaS CEO, just like you. He was 42 pounds overweight, traveling 2 weeks per month, hadn't trained consistently in 3 years.

Every trainer gave him the same cookie-cutter bullshit that fell apart the moment he got on a plane.

6 months later? Lost 42 pounds, squats 2x bodyweight, travels more than ever and stays on track. His exact words: "Marcus is the only trainer who actually gets what my life is like."

The program has 3 phases: Assessment (custom baseline), Rebuild (first 15-20 lb loss), Scale (complete transformation + travel system).

Ready to see if you qualify for one of my 12 spots?

---

#### STEP 7: Application Call Setup

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact expresses interest in qualifying/applying

**Time Delay:** None (immediate response)

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact books application: → Go to Step 13 (Application Booked)
> - IF contact doesn't book: → Go to Step 14 (Booking Follow-up)
> - IF no response after 6 hours: → Go to Step 8 (Follow-up)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: application-ready]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: application-stage]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Excellent. Here's what happens next:

I only work with 12 executives at a time because everyone gets custom protocols and direct access to me. When those spots are filled, they're filled.

To see if you're a fit, fill out this application: [applicationUrl: DATA NOT PROVIDED — DO NOT INVENT]

I'll personally review it and if you qualify, we'll schedule a 15-minute call to discuss your specific situation.

Fair warning: I only accept executives who are 100% committed to following the system. If you're looking for someone to hold your hand while you make excuses, this isn't for you.

Sound good?

---

#### STEP 8: General Follow-up (No Response)

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact hasn't responded to previous message after specified timeframe

**Time Delay:** Wait 24 hours

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact responds: → Return to appropriate step based on conversation stage
> - IF no response after 48 hours: → Go to Step 15 (Final Follow-up)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: follow-up-needed]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Keep current stage]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Hey - just checking if you saw my last message. I know you're busy running a company.

If you're serious about solving this fitness problem once and for all, let me know. If not, no worries - I get it.

But don't let another quarter go by telling yourself you'll "start next month."

---

#### STEP 9: Disqualify (Revenue/Schedule)

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact doesn't meet minimum revenue or schedule requirements

**Time Delay:** None (immediate response)

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - No further automation needed (end sequence)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: disqualified] [-Remove: all-prospect-tags]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: disqualified]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

I appreciate your interest, but based on what you've shared, you're not quite the right fit for The Executive Athlete Program right now.

This system is specifically designed for executives making $300K+ who travel frequently for business. 

I'd recommend starting with [COACH: Insert your lead magnet or lower-tier option here] and we can reconnect when your situation changes.

Best of luck with your fitness goals!

---

#### STEP 10: Re-engagement (Low Engagement)

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact gives short/unengaged responses

**Time Delay:** None (immediate response)

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact re-engages: → Return to Step 4
> - IF contact remains unengaged: → Go to Step 15 (Final Follow-up)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: low-engagement]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Keep current stage]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Look, I can tell you're busy. I get it - I work with CEOs all day.

But here's the thing: if you're not willing to have a real conversation about your health, you're probably not ready to invest $12,000 to fix it.

Are you actually serious about solving this problem, or are you just browsing?

---

#### STEP 11: Value Building (Investment Hesitation)

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact shows hesitation about $12,000 investment

**Time Delay:** None (immediate response)

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact shows renewed interest: → Go to Step 6
> - IF contact still hesitant: → Go to Step 15 (Final Follow-up)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: price-objection]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Keep current stage]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

I get it. $12,000 feels like a lot upfront.

But let me ask you this: What's it costing you to stay where you are? The declining energy that hurts your business performance? The embarrassment at company events? The health risks that could cost you everything?

Rick P. was prediabetic with an A1C of 6.2. His doctor was talking medication. Six months later, A1C dropped to 5.4. His doctor couldn't believe the numbers.

What's preventing a heart attack at 50 worth to you? What's having energy to be present with your kids worth?

The real question isn't whether you can afford $12,000. It's whether you can afford NOT to invest in the one asset that makes everything else possible.

---

#### STEP 12: Objection Handling

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact raises specific objections about program/approach

**Time Delay:** None (immediate response)

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF objection resolved: → Go to Step 7
> - IF objection persists: → Go to Step 15 (Final Follow-up)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: objection-raised]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Keep current stage]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Let me guess - "I don't have time" or "Online coaching can't replace in-person"?

Here's what Dan thought too. He was traveling 2 weeks per month, working 60-hour weeks. But The Performance Stack isn't about finding more time - it's about syncing to the time you actually have.

Twenty minutes between meetings is enough when you have the right system. Dan lost 42 pounds while traveling more than ever because the system worked WITH his schedule, not against it.

The difference between my approach and every trainer you've tried? They gave you generic programs and hoped you'd figure out how to make them work. I give you a system designed specifically for your constraints.

Still think you need someone standing next to you in a gym you'll never visit?

---

#### STEP 13: Application Booked Confirmation

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact successfully submits application

**Time Delay:** None (immediate response)

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - No further automation needed (hand off to sales process)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: application-submitted] [+Add: sales-ready]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: application-review]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Perfect. I've got your application and I'll review it within 24 hours.

If you're a good fit, I'll reach out to schedule our 15-minute call. If not, I'll let you know why and point you toward a better solution.

Either way, you'll hear from me soon.

One thing: when we talk, come prepared to discuss your real constraints and goals. I don't do sales pitches - I do problem-solving. If there's a fit, you'll know. If not, you'll know that too.

Talk soon.

---

#### STEP 14: Booking Follow-up

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact expressed interest in qualifying but didn't book application

**Time Delay:** Wait 2 hours

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact books application: → Go to Step 13
> - IF no response after 12 hours: → Go to Step 15 (Final Follow-up)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: booking-hesitation]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Keep current stage]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Hey - I noticed you didn't fill out the application yet.

Look, I get it. You're busy. But here's the reality: I only have 12 spots and they fill up fast.

If you're serious about solving this problem, take 3 minutes and fill out the application: [applicationUrl: DATA NOT PROVIDED — DO NOT INVENT]

If you're not serious, that's fine too. But don't waste both our time.

The choice is yours.

---

#### STEP 15: Final Follow-up (End Sequence)

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact hasn't engaged after multiple follow-up attempts

**Time Delay:** Wait 48 hours

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - No further automation (end sequence)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: sequence-completed] [+Add: nurture-list]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: nurture]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Last message from me.

I've shown you exactly how executives like Dan and Rick transformed their bodies while building their businesses. I've explained why generic programs fail and how The Performance Stack solves the real problem.

If you're ready to stop making excuses and start getting results, you know how to reach me.

If not, I wish you the best. But please - don't let another year go by telling yourself you'll "start next quarter."

Your health is the foundation of everything else. Treat it that way.

-Marcus

---

### QUALIFICATION QUESTIONS SUMMARY
Based on this sequence, the key qualification criteria are:

1. **Revenue Level:** $300K+ annual income / company revenue
2. **Travel Schedule:** Frequency of business travel (must be manageable within program parameters)
3. **Work Hours:** 60+ hour work weeks (confirms target demographic)
4. **Investment Readiness:** Willing to invest $12,000 for 6-month transformation
5. **Commitment Level:** Serious about following system vs. looking for quick fixes

### MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS
- Male executive age 40-55
- Runs own company making $300K+ revenue
- Has tried 3+ previous fitness approaches that failed
- Willing to invest $12,000 in health transformation
- Committed to following structured system for 6 months
- Travels for business but not more than 75% of time

> The Executive Athlete Program is a 6-month 1-on-1 coaching system that helps executives like you lose 30 lbs without missing a single business meeting.
> 
> • Custom training that works from your home office
> • Nutrition protocols for business dinners and travel  
> • Weekly video reviews and biweekly strategy calls

#### NODE: Online Coaching Answer

- **Delay:** Instant
- **Tags:** None

**Message:**
> Great question. Dan M. was skeptical too - he'd tried 3 local trainers who all failed because of his travel.
> 
> The Performance Stack works better than in-person because it's designed around YOUR schedule, not gym hours.
